title: SACRAMENTO, CA —
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Gun violence is an urgent public health crisis that has brought trauma, injury, and death to many Californians.",
    "AB 1929 will allow California to expand Hospital-Based Violence Intervention Programs, which have proven effective at reducing violence and saving lives.",
    "The COVID-19 pandemic has intensified the country's gun violence crisis.",
    "2020 was one of the deadliest years on record for the United States, with gun homicides and non-suicide-related shootings taking approximately 19,300 lives, a 25 percent increase from 2019.",
    "Early data on 2021 shows it to be on pace as the worst year for gun violence in decades, surpassing even the high levels of 2020.",
    "Studies have shown that up to 41% of patients treated for violent injuries, such as shootings, are re-injured within five years.",
    "A survey of victims of violence found that 20 percent of patients treated for violent injury had died within five years.",
    "The 'revolving door' phenomenon regarding violent injuries is well-documented in the medical literature.",
    "Being the victim of violence significantly increases a person’s likelihood of engaging in violent behaviors against others, often as retaliation.",
    "Hospital-Based Violence Intervention Programs (HVIPs) have been identified as one of the most innovative and effective strategies for reducing rates of violent injury recidivism.",
    "Integrating HVIP services into hospital trauma centers corresponds with large reductions in rates of injury recidivism and long-term increases in patients’ well-being.",
    "A study of the Wrap-Around Project in San Francisco found a 50 percent reduction in the historical re-injury rate over a 10-year period."
  ]
}
```
